summary: Add a bounded in-memory pending-review queue and have MCP mutations publish review records without applying them.

decisions.decision: Bound pending mutations to 64 entries by default, replace duplicate command ids, and remove entries atomically on approve or deny; approval execution remains a later runtime/UI seam.
decisions.rationale: The queue makes review state durable within a session and fail-closed without silently applying external-agent edits or allowing unbounded memory growth.

testEvidence.criterion: MCP mutations publish bounded pending reviews and queue operations remain deterministic.
testEvidence.test: Run focused queue/handler tests once after fixing the interface syntax, lint once, and build once.

red.outcome: RED: the first queue interface used readonly on a method signature; 3 tests passed and the queue suite could not transform.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/mcp/mcp-review-queue.test.ts tests/application/mcp/workbench-mcp-handler.test.ts --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd lint; pnpm.cmd build
green.outcome: GREEN: 2 files and 5 tests passed; lint/source policy passed; TypeScript/Vite build passed with 236 modules transformed.

questions: Desktop review UI, approval-to-runtime-dispatch path, MCP process/toggle integration, and hosted AC-11.b/c evidence remain pending.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
